text:
There once was a village called Whisper Hollow. The people loved their secret garden. Every spring they'd plant new flowers, and every autumn they'd knit new hats.

One peculiar girl named Fern loved owls. So much so she had twenty-seven pet owls. One night she dreamt an owl flew onto a broomstick.

"Aww!" she sighed. "Just hop."

But it didn't. It soared!

"Bloop!"

It flew across the lake.

"Brrrb!"

Into the forest glade.

"Bling!"

She followed—it glittered! White silhouettes against moonlit leaves, calling softly.

Daylight dawned. Just one owl fluffed its feathers sleepily.

"Woow!" said Fern. "You're my morning star!"

It didn't reply. Just shook its head.

Fern felt sad.

"I wish you could talk."

With one last flicker—

"It's morning!" she cried.

And it fluttered away.

They missed owl. Went owl
